LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT LUISA HANSON'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT IS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR FOR NONCOMPLIANCE WITH CCP 430.41. THAT LAW REQUIRES A MEET AND CONFER "IN PERSON OR BY TELEPHONE" BEFORE A DEMURRER MAY BE FILED AND A DECLARATION STATING THE "MEANS" - I.E., IN PERSON AND/OR TELEPHONE - BY WHICH THE MEET AND CONFER WAS HELD. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO COMPLY WITH THE CODE AND HANSEN'S RESPONSE TO THE AMENDED COMPLAINT IS NOW DUE BY MARCH 17, 2022. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: S. LE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/RBU)

Off Calendar Jun-06-2023 Dismissal

Judge: Anne-Christine Massullo; Clerk: S. Le; Proceedings not reported. (610)

Off Calendar Jun-20-2022 Set For Trial On Feb-14-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-09-2022 Continued To Jun-29-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-18-2022 Continued To May-24-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-17-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT LUISA HANSON'S DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

Luisa Hansen's demurrer to the amended complaint is ordered off calendar for noncompliance with CCP 430.41. That law requires a meet and confer "in person or by telephone" before a demurrer may be filed and a declaration stating the "means" - i.e., in person and/or telephone - by which the meet and confer was held. The parties are ordered to comply with the code and Hansen's response to the amended complaint is now due by March 17, 2022.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. EDWARD M. HIGGINBOTHAM, Esq. of Bryant Law Group (Tel: 415-581-0885) for Defendant Louisa Hanson;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT LUISA HANSON'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant Luisa Hanson's unopposed demurrer to the complaint is sustained with leave to amend.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Christine L. Jordan, CSR No. 12262, cjordan@sftc.org (302/RBU)
